text.title: Auto Rollback Policy Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Auto-rollback fires after a single bad inference. Flapping deploys multiple rollbacks per hour.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wired alerting on raw score without rolling window.
antipattern.fix: Rolling window ≥ 100 inferences AND ≥ 5 min. Below either, no trigger.

antipattern.symptom: Deploy goes from 0% → 100% on a button click. Regression hits 100% of users before rollback fires.

root-cause.source: Google SRE handbook; LinkedIn rollback notes

antipattern.root-cause: Pipeline didn't support canary; author thought rollback alone was enough.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory canary ladder 5% → 25% → 100% with 6h hold per step. Auto-rollback within canary stages.

antipattern.symptom: All rollbacks require on-call to type a command. Median rollback time 45 min after threshold breach.
antipattern.fix: Auto-rollback by default; manual override exists for novel failure modes. Log every override + reason.

antipattern.symptom: Rolled-back deploy goes back out unchanged 3 days later — same regression.
antipattern.root-cause: Rollback was treated as the end-state; postmortem was skipped.
antipattern.fix: 4-step runbook: capture-state → notify-owner → freeze-deploys → schedule-postmortem within 5 days.
